steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The blank introduces a noun (“advocate”) that is part of the subject. This noun is immediately followed by a proper noun (“Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez”) that identifies the person—an appositive structure.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content:
Restrictive appositives—those essential to identifying the noun—take no commas.

The name “Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez” is necessary to specify who the lawyer and cycling advocate is, making it restrictive and requiring no commas.

The other three all add commas, incorrectly treating it as non-restrictive.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
